formatted_address: 1457 Garden View Ct, Saratoga Springs, UT 84045, USA
pitch_rationale: The primary home at this Saratoga Springs, UT address displays moderate shadow casting from its eave lines — shadows are clearly defined but not elongated enough to indicate a steep 8:12+ pitch. The ridge-to-eave geometry shows the ridge set well inward from the building perimeter, consistent with a mid-range slope. Multiple hip and gable roof planes are distinctly visible from satellite, suggesting meaningful vertical rise without being dramatically steep. The complex hip/gable combination roof style and northern Utah climate (snow load requirements) both support a 6:12 dominant pitch, which is the most common standard in Wasatch Front suburban construction. Confidence is moderate as image contrast and tree shadows partially obscure some eave edges.
footprint_rationale: Target identified as the large central house in the mid-frame. Bounding box traced at approximately x:360–630, y:440–795 (270×355 px). The roof is L/T-shaped with a complex hip-dominant layout. Applied ~15% cutout reduction for the upper-left recess and internal shadow areas where overhanging trees partially obscure edges. Two main ridge runs (~225 px total), six hip corners at an average of ~70 px each, two to three valley intersections where roof wings join (~135 px), two to three gable rakes (~100 px), and a multi-segment eave perimeter totaling ~620 px. Scale: 1 px = 0.1867 ft; 1 sq px = 0.034849 sq ft. Confidence is moderate-low (0.38) due to heavy tree shadow coverage partially obscuring roof edges, possible misidentification of attached vs. detached structure, and image resolution limitations.
